postcard-001.webp: Please protect the programs that help people who don't have enough food to eat. Please make more donations to the hungry & poor.
postcard-002.webp: We desperately need SNAP! 47 million people — maybe more — are facing starvation and poverty in this great nation of ours — one that supposedly cares about the welfare of all its citizenry where is the justice of granting tax cuts to those that don't need financial help, while denying basic humanity to those that are in dire straits? Please show that you care and support SNAP Angela B. Pittsburgh, PA
postcard-003.webp: Food Safety & Food Security Are our most important Assets. When People Go Hungry, our Country Fails When Food is unsafe & People Become Sick or Die our Country Fails Please Don't Weaken our Food Inspection Process. Please Don't Let our Children Die From Hunger Don't Let our Country Fail Carol K Pittsburgh, PA
postcard-004.webp: If we can conquer space, we can conquer childhood hunger Joe M PGHPA
postcard-005.webp: When will the government decide that the hunger in this country is bad enough for them to step in to help? How can we watch our neighbors struggle + fail and still feel good about our priorities Amy Pgh. PA
postcard-006.webp: Make tracks to save SNAP! Wendy S. Atlanta GA
postcard-007.webp: Secretary Rollins- SNAP must be protected! Anything less is unacceptable! Ellen R. Deerfield, IL
postcard-008.webp: AMERICA is a LAND OF PLENTY we can fund SNAP we MUST fund SNAP C.C. Eaton Wrens, GA
postcard-009.webp: Dear Secretary Rollins, SNAP is needed more than ever! Food costs keep increasing! Families are desperate and are facing food insecurity +hunger. PROTECT SNAP!!! FOOD IS A BASIC HUMAN RIGHT! America is the greatest of nations & should be feeding its neediest of citizens! J.B. San Diego
postcard-010.webp: Protect Trans Kids from HUNGER! Beth G. Illinois
postcard-011.webp: MAHA cannot mean Make America Hungry Again! Do not cut SNAP to 44 million folks! Batsheva, Asheville NC
postcard-012.webp: Taking Farm fresh local food away from School kids is just wrong! Sarah O, KS
postcard-013.webp: "Love thy neighbor" means we cannot let our neighbors to be hungry. We cannot — should not — take food from the poor to give tax cuts to the rich. SNAP is over 50 years old. This is not the time to cut it Save SNAP! Pamela W Cambridge MA
postcard-014.webp: Supporting the SNAP Program supports: farmers the elderly Veterans children single parents and other struggling Americans
postcard-015.webp: Cuts to S.N.A.P HURT: children seniors grocery stores farmers the disabled our friends. our famalies. our neighbors. our communitits. DON'T CUT SNAP -SARAH O. IOWA
postcard-016.webp: The budget backed by your administration is unjust and short sighted. Our government should invest in feeding people, not punishing them.
postcard-017.webp: We'd not need charity if the world were just. Feed the children.
postcard-019.webp: be an american not an american't support our fellow americans & save the S.N.A.P. program! Kyle O. IOWA
postcard-020.webp: FOOD SHOULD NOT BE A PRIVLAGE TAKE ACTION SAVE S.N.A.P.
postcard-021.webp: I want to live in a country where people are caring and empathetic. I want to live in a place where everyone has what they need to thrive, let alone survive. Hunger is a large problem in the us and getting rid of SNAP benefits doesn't help - it makes it worse. I know you've heard this many times but really put yourself in the shoes of someone. You've lost your job due to a misunderstanding and suddenly you can't provide for your family. This is common and I know people like this. Please help them. ♥ Siena J.
postcard-022.webp: The data is clear: SNAP is one of the most effective anti-poverty tools we have. It lifts millions out of poverty each year and supports local economies. It's not just numbers. It's people.
postcard-023.webp: Dear Sec Rollins, MAHA doesn't mean Make America Hungry Again! We must protect SNAP funding, not cut it— for the people who are hungry and the farmers that feed our nation.
postcard-024.webp: FEED THE PEOPLE! Marnie O - Marion IA
postcard-025.webp: USA needs SNAP Don't defund SNAP! The Rosen family Charlotte NC
postcard-026.webp: Keep SNAP in BLOOM! Linda L. Charlotte NC
postcard-027.webp: HOPE Blooms when people are fed. Please preserve SNAP! Eileen V. G'boro NC
postcard-029.webp: Budget cuts will be devastating. Please preserve SNAP! J.J. Jones Matthews NC
postcard-030.webp: Over 47 million people are struggling to feed themselves and their families protect SNAP benefits ! Amon Pedrazas
postcard-031.webp: PLEASE...!! People need to EAT!! PLEASE
postcard-032.webp: Protect SNAP Benefits They are a lifeline for Seniors and Disabled citizens. Make America take care of our citizens in need.
postcard-034.webp: NO ONE DESERVES TO GO HUNGY
postcard-035.webp: How can the "wealthiest nation on earth" let its own citizens stay hungry? Use our resources to feed hungry Americans Expand SNAP - Anonymous, Colorado
postcard-036.webp: A hungry child in the classroom cannot read. Isn't that enough to ensure funding for SNAP? Please act accordingly Jill G.
postcard-037.webp: Protect our children (and seniors) from hunger. Hungry Kids can't learn which leaves them permanently behind. SNAP is over 50 years old + effective. This is not the time to cut it. Save SNAP! Robert G Hollywood FL
postcard-038.webp: Dear Sec. Rollins, My faith calls me to Act for Justice. Cutting SNAP does Not just take FOOD OFF TABLES—it hurts grocery stores, farmers, and com- munities, especially in RURAL areas, making sure people in the US have enough to eat should be a priority! Janyce F, Boca Reton, FL
postcard-039.webp: We are facing a hunger CRISIS in the US. My faith teaches me that we cannot allow our neighbors to suffer. SNAP must be protected at all costs! -Shelby N. Atlanta, GA
postcard-040.webp: WE ❤️ SNAP! IT WORKS. Protect SNAP funding! Quintai B. Thomasville GA
postcard-041.webp: My faith calls me to act for Tzedek, or justice! We are facing an unacceptable hunger crisis in th US. Right now, over 47 million people are struggling to feed themselves and their families. Sharon J. Hudgins Peachtree City, GA.
postcard-042.webp: Secretary Rollins, I'm writing because the proposed USDA budget cuts of $230 billion over ten years will have catastro- phic consequences for people who are hungry and the farmers who feed our nation. NO ONE DESERVES TO BE HUNGRY SNAP must be protected. Anything less is unacceptable. Thanks, Martha H., Chicago, IL
postcard-043.webp: Supporting the SNAP Program supports: farmers the elderly Veterans children single parents and other struggling Americans
postcard-044.webp: We are facing an unacceptable hunger crisis in the United States. Right now, over 47 million people are struggling to feed themselves and their families. SNAP must be protected! Eden Chicago, IL
postcard-045.webp: be an american not an american't support our fellow americans & save the S.N.A.P. program! Kyle O. IOWA
postcard-046.webp: Dear Secretary, My faith speaks to me to provide help to those vulnerable in our society. The greatness of our country should show that also. Keep Snap. Irene ♥
postcard-047.webp: Hello - No cuts to SNAP! We need it to survive!! Alyona F. Minneapolis
postcard-048.webp: NO ONE should have to choose between eating and paying the bills. STOP THE CRUELTY! From a believer of food security for ALL, Carrie B- Minnetonka, MN
postcard-049.webp: Dear Secretary, Help America by providing food for success. Do the Right Thing Emma, Minot
postcard-050.webp: Hungry kids can't learn! No cuts to food assistance programs!
postcard-051.webp: To: Brooke Rollans No one deserves to be hungry! There should be no disparity between those who do and do not have the proper resources. -Charlotte Berlin
postcard-052.webp: Please I Dont Want any Budy to be huNgrey Sophie
postcard-054.webp: My faith calls me to act for Tzedek, or Justice. We cannot let our people STARVE!!! Susan P. Cleveland, OH
postcard-055.webp: Dear Secretary Rollins, As a mom, nutrition is crucial for children to have for growth. It is my responsibility, as a mom and Jew, to ensure that all children and family have what they need. Please protect SNAP!!! Sincerely, Sarah Sobel (OH)
postcard-056.webp: I want my tax dollars to support SNAP benefits for those in need! We all want a healthy country. Maintain SNAP. - Ohio Voter
postcard-057.webp: Roses are fed by the generously shared waters of the sky. Why not be one of those clouds, and contribute to the growth of a beautiful part of the community? - Jin Litman
postcard-058.webp: Protect trans kids from hunger! SNAP must be protected! please help!!! - Jacqueline H.
postcard-059.webp: "Waste, Fraud and Abuse" aren't ramp- ant, your lies are! Give back SNAP benefits to the poor! They need them to survive. Megan S. Chicago, IL
postcard-060.webp: Hunger is not a partisan issue! Protect SNAP and other programs that help children, seniors, and veterans eat with dignity. Wendy K. Pittsburgh PA
postcard-061.webp: Dear Secretary Rollins, Right now, over 47 million people are struggling to feed themselves and their families. Any cuts to SNAP/WIC will be devastating to our most precious resource— OUR CHILDREN. No one should go hungry in this country! Do NOT CUT USDA FOOD PROGRAMS. Put our CHILDREN AND OUR FUTURE FIRST. fighting despair in the PN W. Lorraine B. Olympia, WA
postcard-062.webp: Secretary Brooke Rollins, SNAP saves lives! * Children Elderly Disabled Families Poor Vulnerable In the wealthy US we must provide adequate nutrition for all. Jufgang
postcard-063.webp: Do our children, mothers, fathers, grandparents, students, deserve to be hungry? Why & how can we allow this to happen? No one desenes to go & be hungry -Chloe
postcard-064.webp: Secretary Rollins, Join us at our local food bank to see how many people rely on our federal assistance to feed their kids. Cutting SNAP hurts us all. Protect and Fund SNAP. Laurie d. North Bend WA
postcard-065.webp: I am writing because people need food, and are suffing from starvation. SNAP is one of the best food support programs we have, to help people in need. SNAP NEEDS to continue to support people struggling with financial problems. DONT cut off funding! - JSeeera
postcard-066.webp: Please Save SNAP Feed the Americans That Need It! Thank You, Bethany Gordon Woodinville, WA
postcard-067.webp: Our local food bank ensures that families can eat, children have the nutrition to learn & grow and our communities stay healthy! Protect support for local food banks and protect SNAP! Rachel C. Maple Valley, WA
postcard-068.webp: Cutting SNAP takes food off tables and has a negative ripple effect - grocery stores, farmers, and rural communities like mine. We must do better. Bert Klimas Stevens County, WA
postcard-069.webp: Value of SNAP?!? Remember the mothers, daughters, sisters, grand- mothers-even some men- who were the "caregivers" homemakers, volunteers, etc.?? Now 70, 80, or older, they are only "entitled" to a few hundred $$ in Social Security (less than $1,000) because they were NOT "Employed"! SAVE SNAP!! These people Need + Deserve SNAP Benefits!! George S. Colville WA
postcard-070.webp: dont keep the hunger keep the SNAP PROGAM Ellen AGE 9 Washington D.C.
postcard-071.webp: 1 in 8 Rely on SNap - My uncle - My Cousin - My Cousin mom - My uncle Don't Cut SNAp - Emilia
postcard-072.webp: Millions of children will go hungry if SNAP is cut. Marizio M. Wash, DC
postcard-073.webp: Snap helps alleviate hunger and poverty. PLEASE preserve SNAP full funding! Katrina J. Myrtle Bch, SC
postcard-075.webp: Food is a human right! SNAP must be protected. Anything less is inhumane. Children need food - it's not their fault. The US has a responsibility to feed its people. Melanie J - Charleston, SC
postcard-076.webp: I want to live in a country where we care for one another, especially in hard times. Noelene P.
postcard-077.webp: SNAP cuts = Job Loss ! Rural grocery stores, and the jobs they provide depend on neighbors spending SNAP benefits. Margaret, Charleston, SC
postcard-078.webp: Food is a human right— everyone deserves access to affordable, healthy, clean food. No one should die of hunger. We can fix this problem. Thank you, Roxy Roads
postcard-079.webp: PRESERVE SNAP for the USA! Joy P. Santee, SC
postcard-080.webp: Hunger Hurts. SNAP HELPS. Please preserve SNAP Lee R. Charleston SC
postcard-081.webp: SNAP must be protected! DO THE RIGHT THING! A.H. Green Seneca SC
postcard-082.webp: Dear Madam Secretary, My faith calls me to speak out againt the USDA budget cuts and the gutting of SNAP. The SNAP program lifts millions out of poverty and supports the rural / agricultural areas of the US. The hungry need SNAP and so do the farmers! A volunteer, Gail Bend OR.
postcard-083.webp: Dear Ms Rollins; I work with many homeless individuals in Bend Oregon and witness their despair, hopelessness, pain and hunger. SNAP is a basic simple way to help fellow Americans. This is why I pay taxes, to help, not to further inflict pain. Respectfully, Collie
postcard-084.webp: Honorable Secretary Rollins, We are now facing an unacceptable hunger crisis in our United States. Right now, over 47 million people are struggling to feed themselves and their families. USDA budget cuts of $230 billion in the next decade will have catastrophic consequences for hungry people and farmers. Mark S. Hillsboro OR
postcard-085.webp: Dear Secretary Rollins, I am writing because my faith calls me to speak out with love. I want my country to aspire to generosity and care. We must do all we can to assure that SNAP is protected. Anything else is unacceptable! Please do all you can to STOP the budget cuts that will have catastrophic consequences for people who are hungry. Thank you! Heather (volunteer)
postcard-087.webp: To eat or not to eat That should not be a question Every one derserves to E A T
postcard-088.webp: Hunger is Real Hunger Hurts Hunger Can Be Stopped Fund SNAP! Augusta L. Houston TX
postcard-089.webp: what you do matters. St. Stcin
postcard-090.webp: HUNGER IN THE USA IS UNACCEPTABLE SNAP IS THE ANSWER! Augusta L. Houston TX
postcard-091.webp: Have a heart and feed the children, so they don't have to sleep with an empty belly and an aching stomach.
postcard-092.webp: Kids shouldn't have to wonder about where their next meal is coming from... Instead they should be wondering where they should go play at with friends. Take care of our kids more in the USA first!! - Elaine M.
postcard-093.webp: Over 47 million People are Struggling To Feed Themselves and Their Families Protect SNAP Benefits ! Amon Pedrazas
postcard-094.webp: PLEASE...!! People need to EAT!! PLEASE
postcard-095.webp: Protect SNAP Benefits They are a lifeline for Seniors and Disabled citizens. Make America take care of our citizens in need.
postcard-097.webp: NO ONE DESERVES TO GO HUNGY
postcard-098.webp: How can the "wealthiest nation on earth" let its own citizens stay hungry? Use our resources to feed hungry Americans Expand SNAP - Anonymous, Colorado
postcard-099.webp: A hungry child in the classroom cannot read. Isn't that enough to ensure funding for SNAP? Please act accordingly Jill G.
postcard-100.webp: Secretary Rollins, "Have you no decency?" Starving children is disgraceful & immoral! Wendy A.
postcard-101.webp: Our children in this country should be put first. If you have the power to help these children then DO SOMETHING! Don't let these children go hungry under your watch! Cale & Pah DA
postcard-102.webp: Tina P PGH PA There is enough food on this Planet for everyones needs but not for everyones greed Feed the Kids!
postcard-103.webp: As an educator, I know first hand that children who don't have their basic needs met aren't available for learning. Our youngest citizens — and all Americans — deserve better. Please protect snap benefits. With hope and gratitude, Kate L. Pittsburgh, PA
postcard-104.webp: Secretary Rollins — By depriving young scholars FOOD, you deprive them of an education, of a full, rich life. Do unto others as you would have them do unto you. Julia Portland ME
postcard-105.webp: Dear Ms Rollins, Please do not cut programs that provide food to food pantries. I volunteer at a food pantry + see the need in person. We have all ready seen a drop in the food we can give the people we serve. Thank you for attention to this important matter. Sincerely, Nancy Osborn Elkins Park, PA
postcard-106.webp: Mama said "go out and get the milk" and I say "Mama we don't got no cow." and Mama says "No, go to the store and get the milk" and I say "Mama we don't got no store!" Sally Stroudsburg PA
postcard-108.webp: NO one should have to worry about where their next meal is coming from! Beth S. Pittsburgh, PA
postcard-109.webp: Dear Madam Secretary, Don't turn a deaf ear as people around the country and around the world cry out and perish for want of food and compassion. With so little effort, you could restore them and save the lives of millions! Give back the funding that allows Mazon to support the needy among us. Sincerely, Rabbi Peg K. New Britain, PA 18901
postcard-110.webp: Many families depend on benefits to feed their families. As someone who sees the need of pantries, it also shows the need for appropr- iate SNAP benefits. These benefits NEED TO BE PROTECTED
postcard-111.webp: Dear Secretary Rollins, There is an unacceptable hunger crisis in the United States. Over 47 million people are food insecure, including children, seniors & people with disabilities. The US needs to do more than meet basic needs. I want our country to aspire to generosity & compassion. - Nomi L., East Williston NY
postcard-112.webp: To Secretary Brooke Rollins Protect SNAP Restore Funding Feed People Have compassion! Julie, NY
postcard-113.webp: ATTN: Brooke Rollins Food is a basic human right. There should be zero debates about feeding hungry humans. Period. Tosha Hawkin
postcard-114.webp: No one should ever have to worry where their next meal is coming from. Keep it so that doesn't happen From, Lily F
postcard-115.webp: NO ONE should have to choose between eating and paying the bills. STOP THE CRUELTY! From a believer of food security for ALL, Carrie B- Minnetonka, MN
postcard-116.webp: It's a shame that military families are at risk of hunger! No cuts to SNAP. Dana Fox, Minneapolis
postcard-117.webp: Hungry kids can't learn! No cuts to food assistance programs!
postcard-118.webp: My faith calls me to act for justice. I am calling on my country to do more for our neighbors who face food insecurity. We must be generous and care for those in need. PROTECT SNAP! Beth M Ann Arbor, MI
postcard-119.webp: Keep SNAP!! Keep SNAP!! Keep SNAP!! Keep Food on the tables of low income families!! F. Jamer Madison, AL
postcard-120.webp: I am writing because we are facing an unacceptable hunger crisis in the United States. Right now, over 49 million people are struggling to feed themselves and their families. This is important to me because my faith calls me to act for justice. SNAP must be protected — anything less is unacceptable Marilyn L. Madison, AL
postcard-121.webp: SNAP must be funded S NAP must be funded! SNAP must be funded SNAP must be funded SNAP must be funded SNAP must be funded Arielle J Baltimore MD
postcard-122.webp: Most of the people facing hunger in the U.S. are children, seniors or people with disabilities. SNAP must be protected and maintained. Steven F., Rockville, MD
postcard-123.webp: To: Brooke Rollans No one deserves to be hungry! There should be no disparity between those who do and do not have the proper resources. -Charlotte Berlin
postcard-124.webp: Please I Dont want any Budy to be hungrey Sophie
postcard-126.webp: Secretary Rollins, SNAP helps millions of people access food. They're people - not just numbers! SNAP must be protected - anything less is unacceptable. Barbara M. Tempe, AZ
postcard-127.webp: Dear Secretary Rollins, I am writing because we are facing an unacceptable hunger crisis in the US. Right now, over 47 million people are struggling to feed themselves and their families. This is important to me because my faith calls to act for Tzedek, for justice. and I volunteer at a food pantry and I see the deep need for these programs Miriam H. Montclair NJ
postcard-128.webp: I am writing because people are struggling to feed their families. Budget cuts will have drastic consequences for people who are hungry and the farmers who feed our nation. Marsha L. Florham Park, N.J.
postcard-129.webp: I am writing because we are facing an unacceptable hunger crisis in the US. Right now, over 47 million people are struggling to feed themselves & their families. - Aaron N.
postcard-130.webp: Food Stamp/SNAP grants have been reduced to amounts that do not address need. Grants need to be increased to Clinton-era equivalents to sustain our population. I worked for the Dept of Public Welfare until my retirement, and suggest that all households be found eligible, with the amount of the grant tied to income, resources and ability to work. Neala Freedman
postcard-131.webp: Dear Secretary Rollins, SNAP works & people who are fed also work. Protect SNAP and more people will have the energy to get to work and - as you say you want - can make America Great Again! Be smart, support SNAP. Bruce L, Canadensis, PA
postcard-132.webp: My faith calls me to act for Tzedek, or Justice. We cannot let our people STARVE!!! Susan P. Cleveland, OH
postcard-133.webp: Dear Secretary Rollins, As a mom, nutrition is crucial for children to have for growth. It is my responsibility, as a mom and Jew, to ensure that all children and family have what they need. Please protect SNAP!!! Sincerely, Sarah Sobel (OH)
postcard-134.webp: I want my tax dollars to support SNAP benefits for those in need! We all want a healthy country. Maintain SNAP. - Ohio Voter
postcard-135.webp: Roses are fed by the generously shared waters of the sky. Why not be one of those clouds, and contribute to the growth of a beautiful part of the community? - Jin Litman
postcard-136.webp: SNAP lifts millions of people out of poverty each year and supports local economies. Please protect SNAP! Erin R Chicago IL
postcard-137.webp: Protect trans kids from hunger! SNAP must be protected! please help!!! - Jacqueline H.
postcard-138.webp: "Waste, Fraud and Abuse" aren't ramp- ant, your lies are! Give back SNAP benefits to the poor! They need them to survive. Megan S. Chicago, IL
postcard-139.webp: We're not KITTEN around! we want you to preserve SNAP! Francine F. Mooresville NC
postcard-140.webp: "By the people, of the people, and for the people," is what the credo states. Taking away necessary programs does not fulfill the premise. Do not cut the vital SNAP program! David Leaper Weaverville, NC
postcard-141.webp: I am appalled by the un- acceptable hunger crisis in the U.S. Over 47 million people are struggling to feed themselves and their families. SNAP must be protected as it lifts many out of poverty and sup- ports local economies. Every world religion teaches us to help those in need. Please do your part to help. Donna A. Greensboro NC
postcard-142.webp: Dear Secretary Rollins, Please protect trans kids like me. We are all human and we deserve support!!
postcard-143.webp: To Brooke Rollins, The thought of having SNAP benefits cut is not only incredibly sad but its unethical. Families like mine, a single parent household, rely on these benefits to feed our children. You have an opportunity to do the right thing and continue your duty of servicing the publics need. I hope you make the right choice. - Cierra, former SNAP recipient
postcard-144.webp: Hunger is not a partisan issue! Protect SNAP and other programs that help children, seniors, and veterans eat with dignity. Wendy K. Pittsburgh PA
postcard-146.webp: Secretary Brooke Rollins, With great power comes great responsibility. Please do not cut programs that feed millions of Americas every day. Please use your power to protect our nation's most vulnerable. — Jennifer D. Charleston, SC
postcard-147.webp: SNAP is RAIN in food Deserts. Without SNAP, many rural grocery stores would not be able to stay open, leaving whole communities in food deserts. MB - Chas. SC
postcard-148.webp: The average SNAP recipient receives $6 per day. & most are employed. SNAP is necessary when companies are allowed to pay workers less than a living wage. Punish the companies taking advantage - not the hard working people. Melenie J - Charleston, SC
postcard-149.webp: Dear Secretary Rollins, I work at a food bank, and I see the deep and vital need for programs like SNAP. For every one meal a food bank provides, SNAP can provide nine. SNAP must be protected, not cut. Anything less is unacceptable. Sincerely, Kara M. Charleston, SC
postcard-150.webp: Dear Secretary Rollins, I am writing because I grew up hungry. My single mother and disabled grandmother worked hard to care for 4 children. SNAP was the only thing between us and extreme poverty. Please don't take away this vital and necessary program that helps so many hardworking families and innocent children. I work for a food bank now to give back and see the devasting effects of hunger. The data is clear that SNAP is the most effective anti-poverty tool. Please do the right thing. Pleading, Misty B. Summenille, South Carolina
postcard-151.webp: Dear Secretary Rollins At least PRETEND that Jesus's commandment to feed the hungry matters to you! DO IT! Bethany Crane Lake, WI
postcard-152.webp: No one deserves to go hungry! To have food is not negociable! Maria B. Madison, WI
postcard-153.webp: I am writing because a population that I care about faces higher rates of food insecurity due to discrimination and lack of support. This is important to me because I want to live in a country where we care for one another, especially in hard times. Delaney H. Washington, DC
postcard-154.webp: Most of the people facing hungar in the U.S. are children, seniors, or people with disabilities. They are often our most vulnerable neighbors — those least able to absorb cuts to federal food assistance programs. It's not just numbers. It's people. — McKenna M.
postcard-155.webp: I am writing because SNAP is one of the most effective anti-poverty tools, lifting millions out of poverty each year. while also supporting local economies. In a country that has so much, food is something we can provide. Sara A. Phoenix, AZ.
postcard-156.webp: Food is a human right— everyone deserves access to affordable, healthy, clean food. No one should die of hunger. We can fix this problem. Thank you, Roxy Roads
postcard-157.webp: No one should go hungry in the richest country in the world to benefit the rich. Do not cut SNAP benefits to the needy. Get a conscience! Norman K Phoenix, AZ
postcard-159.webp: SNAP SNAP SNAP SNAP SNAP SNAP SNAP SNAP SNAP SNAP snap benefits for seniors MATTERS Theresa Ry[nolds?]
postcard-160.webp: Brooke: I'm not able to feed my kids without SNAP. HELP! - Becky N.
postcard-161.webp: In the richest land on earth, No child, No elder, No neighbor Deserves to go Hungry! No exceptions! No excuses! Patricia O Mercer Island, WA
postcard-162.webp: My grandma doesn't have enough food without SNAP That's Not ok! Jeanne M.
postcard-163.webp: OH SNAP! You gatta feed people!! Liz X.
postcard-164.webp: Kids shouldn't have to wonder about where their next meal is coming from... Instead they should be wondering where they should go play at with friends. Take care of our kids more in the USA first!! - Elaine M.
postcard-165.webp: Didn't you get into politics to help others? Kim M.
postcard-166.webp: I get $946 a month in Social Security. My rent is $840 - I have only $6 left after I pay bills. I was getting $186 in food stamps. I run out of food every month. Betty R age 76
postcard-167.webp: Brooki Do right by your constituents + approve food benefits Tcry Ro
postcard-168.webp: Everyone Should have acess to nutritious food ♥
postcard-169.webp: Food is a basic need Do the Right thing Doc Rjes
postcard-170.webp: Everyone needs food Security. Lets be there for eachother♥ johnaees Wilson
postcard-172.webp: Brooke, please don't let people starve. Support food benefits. thank you ♡ aleylexi
